mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate above 5% constrains corporate borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates. This monetary tightening regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.33% indicates active tightening cycle
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor market conditions that sustain consumer spending and corporate earnings growth. This full-employment regime produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings momentum remains intact.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.8% indicates tight labor market
regime_hint: full employment regime with UNRATE sub-4.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE < 4.0%, or correlation between UNRATE and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 3% year-over-year while housing starts fall below 1.4 million units annualized, signaling weakening consumer demand and construction activity. This demand contraction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1521 units and RSAFS at 709590M suggest moderate demand
regime_hint: late-cycle demand deceleration with synchronized weakness in consumption and housing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite joint weakness in RSAFS and HOUST, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month while remaining below 103.0, indicating manufacturing weakness and supply-side constraints. This supply contraction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as near-term earnings revisions turn negative.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 102.3313 indicates below-trend production
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances when INDPRO declines and remains below 103.0, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 jobs per month while unemployment rate rises above 4.0%, signaling labor market deterioration. This employment weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as recession risk increases and earnings forecasts decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 158133k and UNRATE at 3.8% show labor market strength
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase with synchronized employment and unemployment weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite joint labor market weakness, or interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.20) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI index rises above 315.0 (year-over-year growth > 3.5%) while federal funds rate remains below 5.5%, indicating real rates are insufficiently restrictive. This monetary policy lag produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market anticipates further rate hikes.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 312.23 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% show inflation-rate mismatch risk
regime_hint: monetary policy lag regime with inflation above target and real rates positive but insufficient
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite inflation-rate mismatch, or interaction term fails to improve model fit (R² improvement < 0.02) over baseline single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 remains below 22,500 billion chained dollars (annualized growth < 2.0%), indicating below-trend economic expansion. This weak growth regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations compress toward GDP growth rates.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 22679.255 is above threshold but near inflection point
regime_hint: below-trend growth regime with GDP expansion decelerating toward stall speed
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods when GDPC1 < 22500, or correlation between GDPC1 level and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.12) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rises above 104.0 while payroll employment grows above 150,000 jobs per month, signaling synchronized supply and labor expansion. This supply-side strength produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as productivity gains support margin expansion and earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 102.3313 and PAYEMS at 158133k show mixed supply-labor signals
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized supply and labor expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite synchronized supply-labor strength, or interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.18) in any 2-year rolling window.
